build_check.description: The project launches with the headless command, exits with code 0, and produces no fatal errors. Returns 0 or 1; the rest of the score is multiplied by this gate.

requirements.description: The player freely explores a wilderness while fog of war hides unexplored terrain and clears permanently around their movement. The minimap must show explored versus unknown areas updating live. Score 0 if the map is fully visible from the start, if fog is a static overlay, or if reveal is only simulated in a separate panel.

Map drawing records discovered terrain types with distinct markers on a minimap or full map, and landmarks can be annotated through player interaction for added value. Score 0 if terrain types are not distinguished, if annotation is only a label, or if the map does not update from exploration.

Food, ink, rope, and health constrain exploration with visible counts and real consequences: food depletes, ink is spent on notes, rope enables cliff traversal, and danger can force retreat. Score 0 if supplies never change, if they are only decorative HUD values, or if running out has no effect.

At least four terrain types are visually and mechanically distinct, such as forest, mountain, river, cave, and ruins. Terrain should affect movement or required tools. Score 0 if terrain is a repeated tile set with labels, if all areas play identically, or if obstacles do not gate traversal.

The wilderness includes active dangers such as wildlife, cliffs, and quicksand that visibly damage or restrict the player, with health and healing constraints. Score 0 if dangers are decorative icons, if health never changes, or if all areas are equally safe.

Returning to town lets the player sell a completed map for credits based on explored area and annotations, then view or buy equipment upgrades. Score 0 if map value is fixed, if selling is only described, or if upgrades cannot be purchased with earned credits.

At least three equipment upgrades have clear gameplay effects, such as boots changing speed, compass previewing terrain, lantern revealing caves, or pack increasing supplies. Score 0 if upgrades are just listed or if their effects are not visible in play.

The cartography loop is playable as one connected expedition: depart town, reveal terrain, annotate a landmark, spend supplies, return, sell the map, and buy an upgrade. Score 0 if the loop is split into separate non-persistent scenes or if exploration does not affect map value.

The full game loop connects title, town departure, exploration, danger, map drawing, return, selling, upgrading, and deeper exploration without restarting. Score 0 if screens do not transition into one another or if player state resets between them.

The title screen uses a parchment, ink, quill, or wilderness-map composition with an interactive play button and visible transition into the expedition. Score 0 if it is a generic title over flat shapes.

The visual style consistently supports cartography and naturalist exploration across terrain, map UI, town, hazards, and character. Score 0 if most objects are generic coloured primitives or reused panels with labels.

Terrain, player, landmarks, dangers, and map interface are recognizable without labels, using parchment styling and terrain detail. Score 0 if the map is a plain grid, if terrain is uniform colour, or if landmarks are only text.

Player movement, fog reveal, map ink filling, annotation, supply depletion, danger hits, town selling, and upgrades animate or update smoothly in gameplay. Score 0 if changes are instant toggles or static before/after panels.
